— Brand Strategy —
Chapter 01
Brand
Audit &
Discovery
Founded 2010
7 Journey Books
3 Audience Segments
20+ Years Leadership
The Vision Wheel — Brand Identity System
What Exists Today

The Vision Wheel was founded in 2010 by Mary Vogen — a guide with over 20 years advising hundreds of millions in investments and charitable legacies. The existing brand carries genuine warmth and creative depth: overlapping translucent circles evoking wholeness, painterly illustrated workbooks for each movement, and a serif wordmark that suggests heritage and care. The brand's soul is already present. What it needs is a unified, elevated system to match the sophistication of its audience.

Brand Strengths

Distinctive framework. The 7 Movements — Faith, Dreams, Gifts, Risks, Mentors, Commitments, Plans — are a proprietary, sequential journey that no competitor can replicate.

Authentic founder story. Mary's background bridges wealth management and soul-level purpose. This is rare and credible.

Rich visual DNA. The overlapping circles motif is instantly ownable and deeply metaphorical — multiple lives, movements, and purposes converging.

Brand Gaps

Positioning clarity. The current site blends coaching, wealth, philanthropy, and spirituality without a clear hierarchy. High-net-worth prospects need a sharper entry point.

System consistency. Color, type, and layout vary across workbooks and web. A unified system builds instant recognition across all touchpoints.

Institutional credibility signals. Family offices and DAF advisors respond to polished, boardroom-ready materials that still carry the brand's warmth.

Competitive Landscape

The Vision Wheel occupies a rare white space between generational wealth advisors (EY, Bessemer Trust — polished, cold, purely financial) and life coaching platforms (BetterUp, Tony Robbins — high energy, consumer-grade). TVW is the trusted guide who speaks both languages fluently: the language of the balance sheet and the language of the heart.

03 / 20
The Vision Wheel — Brand Identity System
Chapter 02
Brand Strategy
Brand Purpose

To give individuals, families, and organizations the clarity and courage to align their lives, leadership, and generosity around a central, deeply held purpose — and to set that purpose in motion before it's too late.

"Without clarity, wealth dissipates. Without vision, teams drift. Without courage, dreams stay dreams."
Brand Promise

We will give you a proven, immersive framework to name who you are, recognize what you've been entrusted with, and discern how to give with purpose — with greater speed and focus than you could find alone.

Brand Vision

A world where every family, every founder, every organization with influence acts from a place of intentional purpose rather than inherited drift — where generosity is strategic and legacy is designed, not accidental.

Positioning Statement

For high-net-worth individuals, families, and mission-driven organizations who carry a vision but have deferred action, The Vision Wheel is the immersive guided journey that transforms deeply held values into a living legacy — through seven movements that align purpose, leadership, and giving into one coherent whole.

Core Values
Courage Before Clarity
Action often precedes complete understanding. We meet people where they are.
Generosity as Strategy
Giving is not a reaction to wealth — it is an expression of identity and purpose.
The Journey, Not the Shortcut
Lasting alignment unfolds through reflection, conversation, and intentional steps.
Faith as Foundation
Every vision begins with trust — in something larger than strategy or circumstance.
04 / 20
The Vision Wheel — Brand Identity System
Chapter 03
Brand Archetype
& Personality
Primary Archetype: The Sage

The Sage seeks truth, illuminates what is already within, and guides others toward their own wisdom rather than imposing answers. The Sage does not perform certainty — they create the conditions for it to emerge. This is precisely what The Vision Wheel does: through seven deliberate movements, it surfaces what clients already know but have not yet named.

Sage brands: TED, Oprah, Stanford, National Geographic. What separates TVW is that the Sage energy is combined with warmth and direct relationship — not broadcast wisdom, but intimate conversation.

Secondary Archetype: The Guide (Mentor)

Where the pure Sage observes, the Guide walks alongside. Mary Vogen embodies this precisely: she has traveled the terrain of wealth, purpose, and generosity herself. She doesn't teach from a lectern — she accompanies. This secondary archetype prevents the brand from feeling distant or academic.

"You already carry the vision.
We help you hear it clearly."
Brand Personality Spectrum
WarmAuthoritative
SpiritualStrategic
ReflectiveAction-Oriented
PersonalInstitutional
Character Traits
Wise Unhurried Courageous Grounded Intimate Purposeful Steadfast Generous
The Brand Is Not…

Prescriptive. Transactional. Performance-driven. Preachy. Corporate. Quick-fix. The Vision Wheel never rushes, never judges, and never replaces the client's own discernment with the advisor's agenda.

05 / 20
The Vision Wheel — Brand Identity System
Chapter 04
Brand Narrative
The Origin

Every person with influence eventually arrives at a threshold: enough resources, enough success — and yet a quiet feeling that something important remains unnamed. Mary Vogen spent two decades in the halls of wealth management, watching brilliant people with extraordinary resources drift away from what mattered most to them. The Vision Wheel was born from that observation: that clarity is not a luxury — it is the precondition for everything else to mean something.

The Story We Tell

Our clients are not lost. They are full — full of purpose, full of experience, full of quiet longings they've been too busy to explore. The Vision Wheel doesn't give people a vision. It helps them hear the one they already carry.

Through seven immersive movements — Faith, Dreams, Gifts, Risks, Mentors, Commitments, Plans — we create the conditions for that vision to surface: through journaling, creative exercises, shared conversation, music, and reflection. Not a workshop. A journey. Not a deliverable. A transformation.

The Manifesto

You have a vision for your life.
You have always had it.

Maybe you've postponed it.
Maybe success got in the way.
Maybe you've been waiting for the right moment
to ask the deeper questions.

The right moment is now.

Not because time is short —
though it is —
but because the people and causes
that need your gifts are waiting.

Your wealth, your leadership, your generosity
can become something more than impressive.
They can become intentional.

That is what The Vision Wheel exists to do:
to set your purpose in motion
with clarity, courage, and care.

Tagline Options
Set Your Purpose in Motion.
Primary — action, momentum, agency
Clarity. Alignment. Legacy.
Institutional — for family office / B2B contexts
The Journey from Vision to Legacy.
Narrative — for long-form and print contexts
06 / 20
The Vision Wheel — Brand Identity System
Chapter 05
Tone of Voice

The Vision Wheel speaks like a trusted advisor who has also sat with their own questions — not from a position of having all the answers, but from a place of having walked the terrain. The voice is warm but not casual, wise but not superior, spiritual but not sectarian, direct but never rushed.

Dimension
We Sound Like…
We Never Sound Like…
Warmth
Generous, present, unhurried. We acknowledge the weight of a moment before we speak into it. "You've been carrying this for a long time."
Cheerful, performative, relentlessly upbeat. "You've got this!" energy belongs elsewhere.
Authority
Grounded in two decades of experience advising real wealth and real lives. We state what we know with quiet confidence.
Boastful, credential-stacking. We never lead with our résumé or use jargon as a status signal.
Spiritual
Faith is foundational but never exclusive. We speak of calling, purpose, and meaning in ways that resonate across traditions.
Preachy, doctrinally prescriptive. The Vision Wheel creates space for belief; it does not impose it.
Urgency
"While there is still time" — we gently surface the cost of indefinite delay without manufacturing anxiety.
Fear-based, deadline-driven, high-pressure. Urgency is a truth, not a tactic.
Sophistication
Literate, considered prose. We write sentences worth reading twice. We trust our audience's intelligence.
Corporate bullet-point language, startup buzzwords, or overly simplified messaging that underestimates the reader.
Language Principles

Use the second person intimately. "You already carry this vision" — not "Clients often find that…"

Prefer motion over state. "Set in motion" beats "achieve." "Unfolds" beats "happens." The wheel always turns.

Honor the interior life. Name what people feel privately: postponed dreams, quiet longing, the weight of unspent purpose.

Vocabulary Guide
Use

Journey · Clarity · Courage · Legacy · Calling · Entrusted · Generosity · Purpose · Alignment · Steward · Discern · Immersive

Avoid

Optimize · ROI · Deliverable · Onboard · Leverage · Pivot · Disrupt · Scale · Hack · Workshop (alone) · Solutions

The Vision Wheel — Brand Identity System
Chapter 06
Messaging Hierarchy

Every audience receives the same core truth — purpose, clarity, and generosity set in motion — but the entry point, vocabulary, and proof points are calibrated to where they stand.

Master Brand Message
Set your Purpose, Vision, and Giving in motion — with greater speed and focus.
Individuals & Families

"You have a vision for your life and your family. The Vision Wheel gives you a proven path to name it, align around it, and live it — before someday becomes never."

Key Proof Points

90% of wealthy families lose their wealth within 3 generations. The Vision Wheel is your family's clarity before the drift begins.

Emotional Hook

The fear of reaching the end with an un-lived vision.

CTA

Start the Journey

Family Offices & DAF Advisors

"Your clients have the resources. The Vision Wheel gives them the roadmap — a structured, immersive process to convert wealth into a documented legacy of purpose and generosity."

Key Proof Points

Only 30% of family offices have a documented philanthropic strategy. TVW's 7-movement framework creates one.

Emotional Hook

The gap between wealth managed and legacy created.

CTA

Schedule a Generational Impact Session

Nonprofits & Organizations

"When your team doesn't share a clear vision, engagement and generosity suffer. The Vision Wheel aligns your leadership, culture, and donor story around a single, living purpose."

Key Proof Points

Companies with unclear values see lower engagement and growth. Alignment precedes impact.

Emotional Hook

The cost of a team that knows the mission but not the meaning.

CTA

Explore Capital Alignment Workshop

08 / 20
— Logo System —

Logo System · 01 of 03

Primary
Logo
Lockup

The full lockup — mark plus wordmark in horizontal configuration — is the primary brand expression. Use it on all key touchpoints where the complete identity should appear: letterhead, email signatures, presentations, web headers, and print collateral.

Three approved color variants ensure legibility and brand fidelity across every background condition.

Variant Selection Guide

CLR — Full color · parchment, white, or neutral backgrounds

WHT — White reverse · navy, dark, or photographic backgrounds

DRK — Dark lockup · single-color print, letterpress, emboss

CLR — Full Color

For use on parchment, white, cream, and light photographic or illustrated backgrounds

WHT — White Reverse

For use on navy, deep teal, and dark or photographic backgrounds

DRK — Dark

For single-color print, letterpress, embossing, and one-color vendor applications

The Vision Wheel — Brand Identity System
09 / 20

CLR — Full Color

Primary mark for app icons, social avatars, embossed letterhead, wax seals, and any application where space is limited to a square or circular frame.

WHT — White Reverse

For use on navy, charcoal, and dark photograph backgrounds. Retains full brand presence without requiring color reproduction.

DRK — Dark

For single-color print, letterpress, hot-stamp foil, laser engraving, and any process where brand colors cannot be reliably reproduced.

Mark at Scale

48px

32px min

Never reproduce the mark below 32px (digital) or 0.5 in / 12mm (print). At small sizes, use only the DRK or WHT variants for maximum legibility.

Logo System · 02 of 03

The
Logo
Mark

The mark — without wordmark — functions as a brand signature in space-constrained or highly recognizable contexts. It carries the full symbolic weight of the Vision Wheel: the wheel, the seven movements, the center point of clarity.

Use the mark alone only when the brand has been clearly established in the context — paired collateral, an established relationship, or a repeated touchpoint.

For first introductions, always lead with the full lockup.

Mark-Only Contexts

· App icons and favicons · Social media avatars and profile images · Embossed stationery · Wax seals and envelope closures · Environmental and wayfinding · Lapel pins and branded merchandise

11 / 20
THE VISION WHEEL · BRAND IDENTITY SYSTEM

Section 04

Logo Usage Guidelines

Clearspace

Maintain minimum clearspace equal to the cap-height of "x" in the wordmark on all sides. No other elements may intrude this zone.

Minimum Size

Print · 1.5 in min

Digital · 32px

Full lockup: 1.5 in (38mm) / 200px minimum. Icon only: 0.5 in / 24px minimum. Never reproduce at smaller sizes.

✓ Always

Use approved Navy, Parchment, or Gold versions on matching backgrounds.

Scale the mark and wordmark proportionally together — never alter their size relationship.

Use icon-only when space is constrained; ensure surrounding context establishes the brand.

Reverse to white on dark or photographic backgrounds when brand colors aren't available.

✕ Never

Stretch, condense, skew, or rotate the mark or wordmark in any direction.

Apply drop shadows, bevels, glows, gradients, or outline strokes.

Recolor with off-brand or unapproved palette colors.

Place on busy patterns or low-contrast backgrounds without a protective container.

Substitute alternate typefaces for the wordmark or tagline setting.

Application Rules

Workbook Coding: Assign one accent per Movement cover. Use 30% and 60% tints for interior divider pages.

Digital UI: Accent colors serve as category tags and progress indicators — Gold is the only CTA/action color.

Print Collateral: One accent + Navy + Parchment per piece. Avoid mixing more than two accents in a single composition.

Ratios: Primary palette = 70% of any composition. Gold ≤ 15%. All accent tones combined ≤ 15%.

THE VISION WHEEL · BRAND IDENTITY SYSTEM

Section 06

Typography System

Display · Titles & Subtitles

Brilon Regular

Regular · 400

The Vision
Wheel

44px · Cover titles, page headers, chapter openers

Headings · Subheadings

EB Garamond Semibold

Semibold · 600

Clarity Before Action

Seven Movements · One Coherent Whole

18–24px · Section headings, callout titles, sidebar labels

Body · UI · Captions

Montserrat Regular

300 · 400 · 500

The Journey is not a checklist. It is a living encounter with the person you were created to become — and the generous life you were called to build.

CLARITY · ALIGNMENT · LEGACY

13–15px body · 9–11px navigation, labels, eyebrows

Type Scale

Role Face Size
Cover TitleBrilon · Regular44–56px
Page HeaderBrilon · Regular34–40px
Section HeadingEB Garamond · Semibold20–28px
SubheadingEB Garamond · Semibold Italic16–20px
Body CopyMontserrat · Regular13–15px
Pull QuoteCormorant Garamond · Italic22–30px
Eyebrow / LabelMontserrat · Medium UC9–11px
Caption / NoteMontserrat · Light9–11px

Pairing Logic

Brilon's distinctive letterforms establish identity at a glance. EB Garamond Semibold bridges display and text, carrying authority without weight. Montserrat's geometric clarity holds the page at reading size — grounded, accessible, unhurried.

Line Height & Spacing

Display: 1.05–1.15 · Headings: 1.25–1.35 · Body: 1.70–1.80 · Labels: 1.4. Never set body copy tighter than 1.5 — generosity of space is a brand value.

15 / 20
THE VISION WHEEL · BRAND IDENTITY SYSTEM

Section 07

Imagery & Visual Direction

Photography Principles

Natural Light, Warm Tones

Prefer golden-hour and window light over studio flash. Warmth communicates trust, intimacy, and the unhurried quality of meaningful conversation.

Human, Contemplative, Mid-Action

Capture people in thought, writing, or quiet dialogue — not posed or artificially celebratory. The brand lives in the moment before the breakthrough.

Intergenerational & Diverse

Show multiple generations together — especially in family and legacy contexts. The work is about generational transmission, not individual achievement alone.

Environmental: Purpose Spaces

Journals, open books, handwriting, vintage maps, compass, candle light, wood grain — tactile objects that evoke depth of thought and intentional living.

Illustration & Texture

Painterly Wash Backgrounds

Thin, translucent watercolor-style textures in Parchment and Amber tones anchor the workbook interiors and event materials — evoking a handcrafted, journalistic quality.

Fine-Line Iconography

Use 1px weight, single-color line icons aligned to the orbit motif — circular paths, directional arrows, and abstract wheel geometries. Never filled; always outlined.

Paper Texture Overlays

Subtle grain applied at 8–15% opacity over photography and solid-color backgrounds adds depth without distracting. Avoid digital-looking gradients or flat vector fills.

Never Use

Oversaturated, filter-heavy stock imagery

Clichéd "success" imagery (fists, trophies)

Cold blue-toned or clinical photography

Cartoon, emoji-style, or flat 2D illustration

Section 10

Presentation & Environmental

Slide Deck — Title Slide

The Vision Wheel

Generational Impact:
The Family Journey

A 7-Movement Framework for Families of Significance

16:9 widescreen · Keynote & PowerPoint master available · Never use slide builder defaults

Retreat & Event Environment

Table Materials

Journey Book at each seat (Movement-coded). Kraft envelope with handwritten name in Gold ink. Navy runner on tables.

Signage System

Foam-core or acrylic panels: Parchment background, Navy and Gold type. Orbit mark at 3-inch minimum. 18×24 in portrait orientation.

Stage & Screen

Navy fabric backdrop with projection. Gold gobo lighting for Orbit mark. Candle light preferred for intimate retreat settings.

Slide Layout System

01 · Title

Parchment bg · Navy headline · Gold accent line · Orbit watermark at 10% opacity

02 · Section

Navy bg · White Cormorant headline · Gold number · Movement accent

03 · Content

Parchment bg · EB Garamond body · Max 3 points per slide · No bullet points

04 · Quote

Navy bg · Italic Cormorant 36px · Gold quotation mark · Attribution in Montserrat Light

THE VISION WHEEL · BRAND IDENTITY SYSTEM

Section 11

Brand Governance

Launch Roadmap

1

Foundation (Weeks 1–4)

Finalize logo selection. Commission final vector files. Build design token library in Figma. Update website favicon and OG images. Order letterhead and business cards.

2

Digital Rollout (Weeks 5–10)

Relaunch website with new visual system. Update all social profile images and cover art. Launch email template library. Deploy presentation master files.

3

Print & Workbooks (Weeks 8–16)

Redesign seven Journey Book covers with Movement color coding. Produce new stationery suite. Print retreat signage and table materials for next cohort.

4

Full Expression (Month 6+)

Environmental branding for retreat spaces. DAF/family office pitch materials. Brand ambassador kit for organizational partners. Annual brand audit.

Asset Management

Brand Portal

Maintain a shared Figma library with all logo files (SVG, PNG, PDF) in all approved color variants. Keep a ZIP archive for print vendors.

Version Control

Date-stamp all logo files. Never distribute "work-in-progress" logo files. Label files with approved version number (v2.0, v2.1) and color variant.

Vendor Guidance

Provide print vendors with this brand book and a one-page quick reference card. Require proof approval for any item using the logo or brand colors.

Brand Review Cycle

Schedule an annual brand audit each January to assess consistency across all touchpoints. Review the following: logo usage in production, color accuracy in print vs. digital, typographic consistency across web and email, imagery alignment with updated audience positioning, and emerging competitor visual moves.

"A brand is a promise. Governance is how you keep it."

"The Vision Wheel is not a product. It is a posture."

Every visual decision should invite people closer to that posture — never distract from it.

The Vision Wheel
